question: How many customers are there in total?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS customer_count FROM customers
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: How many customers are based in the UK?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS uk_customers FROM customers WHERE country_code = 'GB'
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: What is the total revenue across all orders?
expected_sql: SELECT SUM(total_gbp) AS total_revenue FROM orders
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: How many orders were placed in 2026 so far?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS orders_2026 FROM orders WHERE order_date >= DATE '2026-01-01'
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: How many different countries do our customers come from?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT country_code) AS countries FROM customers
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: Which order status has the most orders?
expected_sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS n FROM orders GROUP BY status ORDER BY n DESC FETCH FIRST 1 ROWS ONLY
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: What is the total amount each country has spent?
expected_sql: SELECT c.country_code, SUM(o.total_gbp) AS spend FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.country_code ORDER BY spend DESC
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: Who are the top 5 customers by total spend this year?
expected_sql: SELECT c.full_name, SUM(o.total_gbp) AS spend FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.order_date >= DATE '2026-01-01' GROUP BY c.full_name ORDER BY spend DESC FETCH FIRST 5 ROWS ONLY
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: What is the average order value?
expected_sql: SELECT AVG(total_gbp) AS aov FROM orders WHERE status NOT IN ('cancelled','refunded')
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: What was the monthly revenue for 2026?
expected_sql: SELECT TRUNC(order_date, 'MM') AS month, SUM(total_gbp) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE order_date >= DATE '2026-01-01' GROUP BY TRUNC(order_date,'MM') ORDER BY month
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: Which customers have placed more than 10 orders?
expected_sql: SELECT c.full_name, COUNT(*) AS orders FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.full_name HAVING COUNT(*) > 10 ORDER BY orders DESC
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: Rank countries by revenue and show each country's share of the total.
expected_sql: SELECT c.country_code, SUM(o.total_gbp) AS revenue, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(o.total_gbp) DESC) AS rnk, ROUND(SUM(o.total_gbp) / SUM(SUM(o.total_gbp)) OVER () * 100, 2) AS pct_of_total FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.country_code ORDER BY revenue DESC
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: Show the cumulative revenue by month through 2026.
expected_sql: SELECT month, revenue, SUM(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS cumulative FROM (SELECT TRUNC(order_date,'MM') AS month, SUM(total_gbp) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE order_date >= DATE '2026-01-01' GROUP BY TRUNC(order_date,'MM')) ORDER BY month
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: Which customers have spent more than the average customer?
expected_sql: SELECT c.full_name, SUM(o.total_gbp) AS spend FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.full_name HAVING SUM(o.total_gbp) > (SELECT AVG(cust_total) FROM (SELECT SUM(total_gbp) AS cust_total FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id)) ORDER BY spend DESC
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: What percentage of orders were cancelled or refunded, by country?
expected_sql: SELECT c.country_code, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN o.status IN ('cancelled','refunded') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) * 100, 2) AS lost_pct FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.country_code ORDER BY lost_pct DESC
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: What is the largest single order value?
expected_sql: SELECT MAX(total_gbp) AS biggest_order FROM orders
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: How many orders are either shipped or delivered?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS fulfilled FROM orders WHERE status IN ('shipped','delivered')
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: What was the total revenue in the first quarter of 2026?
expected_sql: SELECT SUM(total_gbp) AS q1_revenue FROM orders WHERE order_date BETWEEN DATE '2026-01-01' AND DATE '2026-03-31'
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: How many orders were placed by customers in Germany?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS de_orders FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE c.country_code = 'DE'
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: Split customers into four spend quartiles and give the size of each.
expected_sql: SELECT quartile, COUNT(*) AS customers FROM (SELECT customer_id, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY SUM(total_gbp)) AS quartile FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id) GROUP BY quartile ORDER BY quartile
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.

question: What was the month-over-month revenue growth through 2026?
expected_sql: SELECT month, revenue, ROUND((revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month)) / LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) * 100, 2) AS mom_growth_pct FROM (SELECT TRUNC(order_date,'MM') AS month, SUM(total_gbp) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE order_date >= DATE '2026-01-01' GROUP BY TRUNC(order_date,'MM')) ORDER BY month
_note: ILLUSTRATIVE expected_rows against the PROVISIONAL customers/orders schema; recapture on the seeded Oracle DB via the demo dry-run.
